MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Brouillet for and with Karin Smith (Administrator). Peter Myers for and with Karin Smith (for tax issue). Jessica Rowe for and with Warren Jabin (Intestate Heir). Gary Wunderlin appeared by telephone for Ivan Solovieff (Beneficiary). Steve Schroeder for Inheritance Funding. Barbara Jabin (Wife of Warren) also present.

The Court stated that her thoughts on Mr. Schroeder's issue have not changed and that she is upholding the assignment. The Parties are in dispute as to the interest rate Mr. solovieff agreed to pay on the loans (3% versus 5%). With regard to the Minnesota property, Mr. Wunderlin stated that Mr. Solovieff paid for the utilities through the time the property was razed in 2005 and that the agreement was that Mr. Jabin would pay the taxes. Mr. Wunderlin also stated that Mr. Solovieff has no objection to an offset of half of the taxes in the amount of $3,462.00 for 2006 through 2012 and adding to that 3% interest in the amount of $34.62 per year, and that his Client has no utility statements. Mr. Brouillet stated that the only evidence they have is a hand written note dated April 22, 2005 which shows an interest rate of 7.5% on some loans and 5% on the other loan (referred to exhibit L of the petition filed August 30, 2013), that the amounts to be deducted are $33,234.36 for the personal loans, $35,139.30 for taxes which were paid by the decedent over the years and $40,300.00 for the interest in the property Mr. Solovieff already received through the Minnesota court. Ms. Smith stated that there are no funds remaining in Mr. Der's trust account and that 100% of those funds went to Mr. Der's fees. The Parties also presented oral argument/explained time spent to support their positions with regard to the statutory fees, extraordinary fees, and fees for Mr. Kenneys' time/services provided on the issue of value and implications on the partnership interest. The Court stated that Mr. Myers fees should not be part of the statutory fees. Ms. Rowe stated that her Client intends to file a petition for payment of attorney's fees under the common fund theory and asked for the reserve amount to be set at $50,000.00. The Court proposed issuing an order addressing all of the issues and invited Counsel to present a revised order. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-28-2014 Continued To Feb-25-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Broullet and Sean Kenney for and with Karin Smith (Administrator). Jessica Rowe for and with Warren Jabin (Intestate Heir). James Thompson for Tanya Tillman (now deceased/daughter of Nadia Zolotoff. Steven Schroeder for Inheritance Funding Co.

Counsel addressed the issues of the Minnesota property being distributed to Ivan Solovieff from the ancillary proceeding in Minnesota, decrease in Ivan's distribution from this estate/efforts to equalize distribution, the reductions being based on amounts paid by the Decedent and provided by Mr. Wunderlin (Mr. Solovieff's Counsel) for maintenance of the Minnesota property, Mr. Solovieff's personal issues, the difficulty Mr. Wunderlin has with regard to communication with Mr. Solovieff, whether Mr. Solovieff is willing to consent to a reduction, whether Mr. Solovieff has capacity, the request for extraordinary fees, time spent on the issue of the partnership asset, distribution or sale of the partnership asset, Mr. Kenney's analysis of the partnership being explained to all of the Beneficiaries, the reserve amount, timeliness of the assignment, etc. The accounting petition was continued to February 25, 2014 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Wunderlin to appear by telephone regarding contact with Mr. Solovieff and whether he will consent with distribution and whether there is a need for a Special Needs Trust.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Apr-05-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Levon Sagatelyan for Keivan Ehsanipour (Petitioner). Ernest Der for and with Debra Dolch (Special Administrator). Richard Brouillet for and with Karen Jabin Vella. Audrey Gerard for Larry Jabin and 13 Intestate Heirs. Karen Smith and other Family Members were also present.

Mr. Der stated that the bond needed to be increased and that they collected additional assets in the amount of $1,100,000.00 in addition to the real property. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to priority to serve, having a neutral fiduciary appointed and conflict. The Court appointed Karen Smith as Administrator of the Estate of Stanley Jabin with limited authority (subject to a revised order). Bond in the amount of $1,150,000.00 is required. An accounting by Ms. Dolch is to be filed by May 24, 2011.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Whitehead.
